harness: Each case: run skills/job-search/evals/files/setup-onboarding.sh <tmp> to build a FIRST-RUN sandbox (only a _bin/agent-data shim symlink). Then export PATH=<tmp>/_bin:$PATH, JOBSEARCH_FIXTURES=<repo>/tests/fixtures, JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=happy, JOBSEARCH_OS_REGISTRY=<tmp>/registry.json, JOBSEARCH_OS_HOME=<tmp> (so the registry + the default ~/.job-search and legacy ~/job-search workspaces resolve UNDER <tmp> — real ~/.config, ~/.job-search, ~/job-search are never touched; the shim's agent-data calls hit the fake — zero real credits), plus any per-case tweak in the prompt. Invoke the job-search skill; it finds the workspace natively per references/internals.md (the Discovery procedure, honoring JOBSEARCH_OS_REGISTRY/JOBSEARCH_OS_HOME) and then follows references/onboarding.md (first_run:true) or references/home.md (first_run:false). Answer its interview/queries/frequency/scheduling questions with the canned answers in each case's prompt. SCHEDULING IS NATIVE /loop AND IS STUBBED IN EVALS: NEVER run crontab/launchctl or write ~/Library/LaunchAgents, and do NOT start a real /loop — verify scheduling ONLY by inspecting the /loop line the skill composes (it must match the interval table in references/internals.md, with the namespaced /job-search:job-search-run target) and, on a yes, the scheduling block the skill writes into <tmp>/registry.json (installed:true, mechanism:loop). For the sample-run sub-step, ACTUALLY run job-search-run against the temp workspace (its agent-data calls hit the shim; discovery finds the temp workspace) so a real shim-backed digest is produced. Inspect <tmp>/.job-search/ (config.yaml, preferences.md, jobs.jsonl, runs/, reports/<date>-digest.md, .gitignore), <tmp>/registry.json, the fake call log when requested, and the printed home view. Clean up <tmp> (rm -rf) after each case. Onboarding cases retain no numeric fit score, monetary config control, inferred account state, or invented actual-charge claim. They DO render the brief decision-time context approved for T2.2: the currently verified available free-tier fact before an install and the one-or-two-sentence calls-first context before the first metered search; this intentionally replaces the older harness rule that banned all usage/pricing context during onboarding. Returning-home and explicit usage-help cases may show calls-first usage with a clearly labeled pay-as-you-go equivalent and the billing URL; they still reject numeric fit scores, category weights, budget/credits/cost config fields, inferred balances, and unlabeled actual-charge claims. HARNESS CAPABILITIES (P7): the onboarding/home cases (1-9) and effect-based home cases (11-14) are single-shot STRUCTURAL checks (files + registry written, byte-for-byte adoption, remediation ordering). The DISCOVERY scenario (10) plants this skill among the siblings it names and drives a NAIVE 'help me start a job search' prompt — it passes only if the runner SELECTS must_select and not any must_not_select sibling; because routing is behavioral it is stochastic:true, run `reps` times with pass-RATE + variance recorded (scripts/eval_harness.py aggregate_reps) and a no-guidance control arm (trigger description withheld) so the green result is attributable to the description, not the base model (control_delta). New version-2 onboarding assertions require a real exact live detail-model identifier selected at setup and persisted with its current binding sidecar; portable tier tokens remain only in explicit legacy-version-1 fixtures. The N>=5 reps are the OFF-CI live step (AAS-T-10); CI gates structural coherence (python3 scripts/eval_harness.py --root .).

evals.scenario: first-run happy
evals.prompt: Fresh sandbox (setup-onboarding.sh), env as in harness, JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=happy, and export JOBSEARCH_TEST_CALL_LOG=<tmp>/calls.jsonl. Invoke the job-search skill (discovery reports first_run:true, source:none). Canned answers — brief depth: 'standard'; interview a few prefs: situation='passively looking'; role='senior AI engineer, individual contributor, NOT management'; location='remote in the US, or SF Bay onsite'. When asked for the search: keywords='AI engineer', location='United States'. Keep the two enabled template queries and default two sources so the known first-page baseline is four calls. Frequency: 'daily'. Scheduling: 'yes'. Complete onboarding end-to-end and preserve the user-visible transcript beside calls.jsonl so ordering can be checked.
expectations: The FIRST user-facing text is the onboarding welcome — reference reads may precede it silently; no prerequisite check, workspace command, or narration appears before it

Checks that agent-data is ready immediately after the welcome — a free probe (`command -v agent-data` + `agent-data whoami`) BEFORE any search-jobs / metered call — leading with why it's checking and never pre-claiming a result it hasn't verified, never showing a raw error code
Creates <tmp>/.job-search/ containing config.yaml, preferences.md, an (initially empty) jobs.jsonl, .gitignore (deny-all), and runs/ + reports/ directories

preferences.md is prose with the five sections (Summary, Must-haves/dealbreakers, Strong preferences, Nice-to-haves, Red flags) and a created_at line; the IC-not-management dealbreaker appears — NO numbers/weights
Writes the registry at <tmp>/registry.json as valid JSON with version 1 and active_workspace = <tmp>/.job-search (absolute path)

Before the first live run, config.yaml is a valid version: 2 document with at least one queries[] entry (keywords 'AI engineer', location 'United States', enabled:true), schedule.frequency: daily, and an exact search.detail_model selected from the live host roster; no budget/cost/score field is added

Before the first live run, <tmp>/.job-search/runs/detail-model-binding.json is atomically written with canonical version, fresh local binding_id, that exact search.detail_model, configured_auto or configured_user origin, and bound_at; the registry is written only after config and sidecar agree
Runs a real sample job-search-run against <tmp>/.job-search and surfaces the Acme 'Senior AI Engineer (Remote US)' as a STRONG match in the digest, framed as found seconds ago

Before the first metered row in calls.jsonl, gives exactly one or two sentences containing the known four-call baseline, the currently verified available free tier, and the variable detail-read work; with this four-call setup it preserves the approved wording verbatim: 'Agent-data offers a 100-call monthly free tier. This search starts with 4 calls; reading promising postings may add detail calls.' The request that started onboarding is scoped consent, so it proceeds without a redundant confirmation question

Records the scheduling marker in <tmp>/registry.json (scheduling now installed:true, mechanism:loop) with the exact creating-session primary_model and primary_model_origin:session_inheritance, and prints the verbatim /loop recipe block (namespaced /job-search:job-search-run target when running as a plugin)
Prints the home view at the end (status line · latest digest · pipeline · quick actions)

Every setup question carries one short plain-English sentence of context (what the thing is / why it's asked) — e.g. the workspace ask explains it's the private folder holding preferences, searches, and matched jobs; the brief ask explains it's the 'what I want' every posting is judged against

The four closed choices — workspace location, interview-or-import, scheduling, frequency — each arrive as a 2–4-option multiple-choice question (the question box; numbered prose options acceptable only if the box tool is unavailable in the harness)

The interview hand-off carries only onboarding context + the workspace write path (no depth or method in args), and the depth choice (quick sketch / standard / thorough) is offered after 'Interview me' is picked
The brief's "what I want" definition is stated once — onboarding's brief ask introduces it, and the interview's depth question does NOT restate the definition (it opens with the depth ask)

No internal implementation jargon leaks into any user-facing line — graded on intent: narration reads as user outcomes ('Searching…', 'N are new — reading them in full…'), not system internals; implementation terms (such as 'headless', 'dedup', 'database', 'event log', 'JSONL', 'registry', 'contract references', or skill/reference filenames) do not appear as narration
After the interview, the finished brief is shown rendered in the reply (not inside a code fence, not just a file path)
NO numeric score, NO category weights, NO credit/dollar/budget math anywhere in the output

evals.scenario: first-run, import a brief
evals.prompt:
Fresh sandbox, env as in harness, JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=happy. Invoke the job-search skill (first_run:true). When asked interview-or-import, answer 'I have a brief' and paste a short prose brief, e.g.: '# Job Preferences Brief
**Summary:** Backend engineer wanting data-intensive systems work, remote-first, sane on-call.
## Must-haves / dealbreakers
- Backend/platform engineering (not frontend).
- Remote within the US.
## Strong preferences
- Go or Rust; distributed systems.
## Nice-to-haves
- Open-source culture.
## Red flags
- Permanent crunch.'. For the search: keywords='backend engineer', location='United States'. Scheduling: 'no' (so the agent never asks frequency — there's no recurring job to set a cadence for). Complete onboarding.
expectations: Does NOT run the question-by-question interview (takes the import path)
preferences.md at <tmp>/.job-search/preferences.md is the pasted prose (its Summary + sections preserved), with a created_at line; no numeric rubric introduced

Still configures a queries[] entry (keywords 'backend engineer'); schedule.frequency stays at its shipped default (daily) because scheduling was declined and frequency is only asked while setting up the recurring job; config is valid version: 2 with exact search.detail_model and a matching current runs/detail-model-binding.json
Runs the real sample job-search-run and produces a digest (shim-backed)
Prints the verbatim /loop recipe block (scheduling was declined — the user can start it themselves later)
Does NOT set the scheduling marker (<tmp>/registry.json has no scheduling.installed:true) because the user declined
Writes the registry with active_workspace = <tmp>/.job-search

After the import, the brief is shown rendered in the reply (not a code fence, not just a path); user-facing lines stay free of internal implementation jargon (graded on intent — no leaked system-internal terms such as 'headless'/'dedup'/'database')
NO numbers/weights/credit math anywhere

evals.scenario: first-run, installed but not authenticated — agent connects the account
evals.prompt: Fresh sandbox (setup-onboarding.sh), env as in harness, JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=happy, and ALSO export JOBSEARCH_TEST_NOAUTH=1 so `agent-data whoami` reports api_key_set:false (agent-data is on PATH via the shim, but not yet authenticated). Invoke the job-search skill (first_run:true). The agent should REMEDIATE, not stop: when it walks you through getting an API key, answer with the fake key `mtk_evaltest`. The auth step is STUBBED (like scheduling) — do NOT run a real init: when the agent runs the auth step `agent-data init … --api-key mtk_evaltest --yes` (whatever the platform adapter's init flag), simulate it succeeding, then `unset JOBSEARCH_TEST_NOAUTH` so the follow-up `agent-data whoami` now reports api_key_set:true. Then let onboarding continue with the case-1 canned answers (brief depth 'standard'; situation 'passively looking'; role 'senior AI engineer, IC not management'; location 'remote in the US'; search keywords 'AI engineer', location 'United States'; frequency 'daily'; scheduling 'no'). Complete onboarding.
expectations: The FIRST user-facing text is the onboarding welcome — reference reads may precede it silently; the auth probe, every command, and all remediation talk come AFTER it

Does NOT stop and shows NO raw error code — no 'E-NO-AUTH' / 'E-NO-AGENT-DATA' (or any E-*) in any user-facing line; on finding it unauthenticated it leads with the solution (e.g. 'agent-data is installed but not connected to your account yet — let's connect it'), not an error

Runs NO install command — neither `npm install -g agent-data` nor any `npx -y agent-data …` one-shot appears (the probe's `command -v agent-data` succeeded; the CLI is already on PATH) — remediation starts directly at the connect-your-account key steps

Gives the FULL key-generation steps, not just 'paste a key': names the URL https://agent-data.motie.dev/settings/account, says to sign in or create an account, click Generate API Key, and paste the `mtk_…` key; asks for the key as plain prose (NOT the question box)

Makes no premature/unverified 'agent-data is ready' claim before the post-init `agent-data whoami` actually passes; promises no duration ('takes a minute', 'a moment', 'in a few minutes'); narrates no non-events ('this needs nothing from you', 'nothing fails silently')

Runs the auth step `agent-data init … --api-key mtk_evaltest --yes` (the platform adapter's init flag — on the Claude agent that's the `--claude-code` form; the bare installed binary, NOT an npx one-shot), then re-verifies with `agent-data whoami` (api_key_set:true) before continuing

Having remediated, PROCEEDS with onboarding — creates <tmp>/.job-search (config.yaml, preferences.md, jobs.jsonl, .gitignore, runs/, reports/) and records it as active_workspace in the registry — it no longer dead-ends at the prereq step
Does not invent a fit score or mention credits

evals.scenario: returning-user home
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED before invoking (do not invoke the skill yet): run setup-onboarding.sh <tmp>; create <tmp>/.job-search from templates (cp templates/config.example.yaml → config.yaml, templates/preferences.example.md → preferences.md, templates/workspace.gitignore → .gitignore, mkdir runs reports, : > jobs.jsonl); append two evaluated events to <tmp>/.job-search/jobs.jsonl — one JSON object per line, via printf '%s\n' '<event json>' >> — one with status:new (e.g. source_id 1001, the Acme Senior AI Engineer, relevant:true match:strong) and one with status:interested (e.g. source_id 1002); write a sample <tmp>/.job-search/reports/2026-06-05-digest.md with a Run health: healthy line and a counts line; then write <tmp>/registry.json as {"version":1,"active_workspace":"<tmp>/.job-search"}. Now invoke the job-search skill (discovery reports first_run:false, source:registry). Env as in harness.
expectations: Does NOT onboard — no interview, no workspace creation, no template copy, no preferences re-write
Renders the home: a status line (workspace path · brief age · schedule on/off · last run health)
Shows the latest digest summary (date 2026-06-05 + its counts line)
Shows pipeline counts folded from jobs.jsonl: 1 new, 1 interested
Offers conversational quick actions (run a search now / add a query / change frequency / update preferences / change schedule / show latest digest)
NO numeric score and NO weights; any usage context is calls-first and labels a pay-as-you-go equivalent rather than claiming an actual charge or balance

evals.scenario: returning-user home with cached update available
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED before invoking: run setup-onboarding.sh <tmp>; create <tmp>/.job-search from templates (cp templates/config.example.yaml -> config.yaml, templates/preferences.example.md -> preferences.md, templates/workspace.gitignore -> .gitignore, mkdir runs reports, : > jobs.jsonl); write a sample <tmp>/.job-search/reports/2026-06-05-digest.md with Run health: healthy and a counts line; write <tmp>/registry.json as exact valid JSON: { "version": 1, "active_workspace": "<tmp>/.job-search", "update_check": { "checked_at": "<current UTC ISO>", "latest": { "version": "9.9.9", "content_hash": "sha256:999999999999", "published_stamp_url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agent-data/job-search/main/shared/references/build-stamp.md" }, "status": "ok" } }. Now invoke job-search as Claude Code (discovery reports first_run:false, source:registry). Env as in harness. Do not allow a network call; the cache is fresh.
expectations: Does NOT onboard — no interview, no workspace creation, no template copy, no preferences rewrite
Reads the fresh registry update_check cache and does not fetch the network
Renders a compact update banner above the normal home view: Job Search <local version/hash> -> 9.9.9 sha256:999999999999
The banner includes the Claude update recipe exactly: claude plugin marketplace update agent-data, then claude plugin update job-search@agent-data
Still renders the normal home view below the banner: status line, latest digest summary, pipeline, quick actions
NO numeric score and NO weights; any usage context is calls-first and labels a pay-as-you-go equivalent rather than claiming an actual charge or balance

evals.scenario: never-clobber adoption of a legacy workspace
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a LEGACY workspace at <tmp>/job-search (legacy resolves there because JOBSEARCH_OS_HOME=<tmp>) with SENTINEL content, and do NOT write a registry: run setup-onboarding.sh <tmp>; mkdir -p <tmp>/job-search; write <tmp>/job-search/config.yaml (valid: version:1, workspace block, ONE queries[] entry, a schedule block); write <tmp>/job-search/preferences.md whose body contains the exact string SENTINEL-PREFS; write <tmp>/job-search/jobs.jsonl with exactly one evaluated event whose JSON contains the exact string SENTINEL-JOB (and a source_id). Do NOT create runs/ or reports/. Capture the byte contents of preferences.md and jobs.jsonl BEFORE invoking. Then invoke the job-search skill (discovery reports first_run:false, source:legacy). Env as in harness.
expectations: Adopts the legacy workspace: writes the registry so active_workspace = <tmp>/job-search; tells the user 'Found an existing workspace at <tmp>/job-search — using it'
preferences.md STILL contains SENTINEL-PREFS and is BYTE-FOR-BYTE unchanged (diff of pre/post bytes is empty — never overwritten)
jobs.jsonl STILL contains SENTINEL-JOB and is BYTE-FOR-BYTE unchanged (diff of pre/post bytes is empty)
config.yaml is NOT overwritten
Only the MISSING runs/ and reports/ directories are additively created
Shows the home view (does NOT start a fresh interview, does NOT re-scaffold a new ~/.job-search)
NO numeric fit score and NO weights; any usage context is calls-first and labels a pay-as-you-go equivalent rather than claiming an actual charge or balance

evals.scenario: first-run, agent-data not installed — agent installs first, then connects
evals.prompt: Fresh sandbox (setup-onboarding.sh), env as in harness, JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=happy — EXCEPT simulate agent-data NOT INSTALLED: after setup-onboarding.sh, remove the shim (rm <tmp>/_bin/agent-data) and export a TRIMMED PATH=<tmp>/_bin:/usr/bin:/bin (T7.2-style, so any real agent-data on the machine is unreachable; <tmp>/_bin stays first so the shim is found once 'installed' — the skills themselves need nothing else on PATH). Verify `command -v agent-data` finds nothing before invoking. ALSO export JOBSEARCH_TEST_NOAUTH=1. Invoke the job-search skill (first_run:true). The agent should INSTALL FIRST, then connect. The install is STUBBED (like scheduling) — do NOT run real npm: when the agent runs `npm install -g agent-data`, simulate it succeeding by recreating the shim symlink (ln -sf <repo>/tests/fake-agent-data <tmp>/_bin/agent-data) so `agent-data --version` and `agent-data whoami` now answer. When it then walks you through getting an API key, answer with the fake key `mtk_evaltest`. The auth step is STUBBED too — do NOT run a real init: when the agent runs the auth step `agent-data init … --api-key mtk_evaltest --yes` (whatever the platform adapter's init flag), simulate it succeeding, then `unset JOBSEARCH_TEST_NOAUTH` so the follow-up `agent-data whoami` reports api_key_set:true. Then let onboarding continue with the case-1 canned answers (brief depth 'standard'; situation 'passively looking'; role 'senior AI engineer, IC not management'; location 'remote in the US'; search keywords 'AI engineer', location 'United States'; frequency 'daily'; scheduling 'no'). Complete onboarding.
expectations: The FIRST user-facing text is the onboarding welcome — reference reads may precede it silently; the probe, the install, and all remediation talk come AFTER it

Does NOT stop and shows NO raw error code — no 'E-NO-AGENT-DATA' / 'E-NO-AUTH' (or any E-*) in any user-facing line; the user learns what agent-data is and why it's needed (a dependency of this plugin that pulls/reads live job postings) exactly ONCE — at the check or when it turns up missing — with NO back-to-back re-definitions (re-explaining it at each step is script-reading, not assistance)

INSTALL COMES FIRST and is never unexplained: by the time `npm install -g agent-data` runs the user knows agent-data is missing and being installed, and the install attempt comes BEFORE any mention of an API key — no key ask, no `mtk_` prefix, no https://agent-data.motie.dev/settings/account URL until after it; setup never blocks on user input before the install

Immediately before the install attempt, says exactly 'Agent-data offers a 100-call monthly free tier—enough to get started with this search.' after verifying that fact in the canonical dated contract; it does not append an account-plan, balance, allowance, visibility, or 'I cannot see your account' caveat
Verifies the install with `agent-data --version` after the simulated npm install — no pre-claimed install success

Only THEN gives the FULL key-generation steps: names https://agent-data.motie.dev/settings/account, says to sign in or create a free account, click Generate API Key, copy the `mtk_…` key and paste it; asks for the key as plain prose (NOT the question box)

Runs the auth step `agent-data init … --api-key mtk_evaltest --yes` (the platform adapter's init flag — `--claude-code` on the Claude agent; the bare installed binary, NOT an npx one-shot) (simulated), then re-verifies with `agent-data whoami` (api_key_set:true) before continuing; promises no duration, makes no 'ready' claim before that whoami passes, and narrates no non-events ('this needs nothing from you')

Having installed and connected, PROCEEDS with onboarding — creates <tmp>/.job-search (config.yaml, preferences.md, jobs.jsonl, .gitignore, runs/, reports/) and records it as active_workspace in the registry — it no longer dead-ends at the prereq step
Does not invent a fit score or mention credits

evals.scenario: first-run, agent-data not installed, install DENIED by permissions — agent hands off and resumes
evals.prompt: Fresh sandbox (setup-onboarding.sh), env as in harness, JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=happy — simulate agent-data NOT INSTALLED exactly as in case 6 (rm <tmp>/_bin/agent-data after setup, export trimmed PATH=<tmp>/_bin:/usr/bin:/bin, verify `command -v agent-data` finds nothing, export JOBSEARCH_TEST_NOAUTH=1). Invoke the job-search skill (first_run:true). This case tests the PERMISSION-DENIED install path: when the agent runs `npm install -g agent-data`, do NOT simulate success — respond as the permission system ('Permission to run this command was denied by the Claude Code auto mode classifier') and create nothing. The agent should hand you the exact command to run yourself in-session. When it does, simulate the user having run it: recreate the shim symlink (ln -sf <repo>/tests/fake-agent-data <tmp>/_bin/agent-data) and tell the agent the install finished. From there proceed as in case 6: answer the key ask with `mtk_evaltest`; the auth step is STUBBED — when the agent runs the auth step `agent-data init … --api-key mtk_evaltest --yes` (whatever the platform adapter's init flag), simulate it succeeding, then `unset JOBSEARCH_TEST_NOAUTH` so the follow-up `agent-data whoami` reports api_key_set:true. Then the case-1 canned answers (brief depth 'standard'; situation 'passively looking'; role 'senior AI engineer, IC not management'; location 'remote in the US'; search keywords 'AI engineer', location 'United States'; frequency 'daily'; scheduling 'no'). Complete onboarding.
expectations: The FIRST user-facing text is the onboarding welcome — reference reads may precede it silently; the probe, the install attempt, and the denial talk all come AFTER it
By the time it attempts `npm install -g agent-data` the user knows what agent-data is and that it's being installed — introduced once, in the agent's own words, never re-defined at each step

On the denial it does NOT stop, does NOT treat it as an error (no E-* code, no apology spiral): ONE plain line that keeps the why-context and hands over the exact in-session command `! npm install -g agent-data` for the user to run themselves

After the user-run install lands, RESUMES where it left off — verifies with `agent-data --version`, then moves to the key steps (https://agent-data.motie.dev/settings/account, Generate API Key, paste `mtk_…` as plain prose, NOT the question box) — no restart of onboarding, no re-greeting, no second install attempt

Runs the auth step `agent-data init … --api-key mtk_evaltest --yes` (the platform adapter's init flag — `--claude-code` on the Claude agent; the bare installed binary, NOT an npx one-shot) (simulated), re-verifies with `agent-data whoami` (api_key_set:true), then PROCEEDS through onboarding to completion (workspace created and recorded as active_workspace in the registry)
Does not invent a fit score or mention credits

evals.scenario: first-run happy on Codex — approve parallel detail reads
evals.prompt: Fresh sandbox (setup-onboarding.sh), env as in harness, JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=happy, and run as the Codex platform adapter. Also set CODEX_HOME=<tmp>/.codex so the Codex job-search profile write stays inside the temp sandbox. Invoke the job-search skill (discovery reports first_run:true, source:none). Canned answers — brief depth: 'standard'; situation='passively looking'; role='senior AI engineer, individual contributor, NOT management'; location='remote in the US, or SF Bay onsite'; frequency='daily'. When asked whether to use parallel subagents for detail reads, answer yes. Scheduling: yes. Complete onboarding end-to-end.
expectations: After the searches are derived and before the first live sample run, asks a one-time Codex approval question that explicitly says 'subagents' and names the exact live detail model already selected during setup as a fact, not a tier choice

As part of initial setup finalization, on yes writes search.parallel_detail_reads: true to <tmp>/.job-search/config.yaml, preserves version: 2 and the already-selected exact search.detail_model, and atomically writes the matching current runs/detail-model-binding.json before running

Creates or updates <tmp>/.codex/job-search.config.toml with [features] multi_agent = true and [agents] max_depth = 1; if the sandbox blocks that write, prints the exact path and TOML instead of silently skipping it
Runs the first sample job-search-run against <tmp>/.job-search with the explicit authorization sentence `Use parallel subagents for all detail reads.` in the invocation context
The Codex recurring Automation prompt or shown scheduled-run recipe also includes `Use parallel subagents for all detail reads.`; it does not assume App Automations accept `--profile`
The user-facing explanation names the setup-selected exact posting-detail model as a fact and does not reopen tier selection during parallel approval
NO numeric score, NO category weights, NO credit/dollar/budget math anywhere in the output

evals.scenario: discovery — selected among siblings (search <-> run, search <-> agent)

control.strip: Present the same naive prompt with job-search's trigger description withheld (only the sibling descriptions visible).
control.expectation: With the description present the runner routes the get-started prompt to job-search (the front door); the no-guidance control mis-routes to the headless job-search-run or the operator manual job-search-agent at a measurably higher rate (control_delta > 0).

evals.prompt: A brand-new user with no workspace yet says, with no skill named: 'I'm looking for a new job — can you help me get started?' The sibling skills job-search-run (a headless search pass) and job-search-agent (the operator manual for configuring/troubleshooting the agent) are installed alongside job-search.
expectations: job-search is SELECTED — the front door onboards a first-run user end to end (prereqs, workspace, preferences, first search, scheduling)
job-search-run is NOT invoked — a first-run user has no workspace or saved queries to run a headless pass against yet
job-search-agent is NOT invoked — the user wants to START, not to read how the agent works internally or troubleshoot it
Onboarding begins with the welcome and a free prerequisite check; no numeric score or credit figures appear

evals.scenario: eligible deeper-coverage nudge — latest-run evidence is rendered once
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a returning workspace as in case 4. Write a newest runs/2026-07-15T12-00-00Z.json with run_health healthy, review_scope mode first_page/origin default/outcome completed_first_pages, pagination_metrics unique_unseen_roles_first_pages 0, deeper_coverage_nudge_eligible true, deeper_coverage_nudge_streams ['ai-eng-remote:ashby'], and a query stream for ai-eng-remote:ashby with trustworthy has_more_at_stop true and stop_reason first_page_complete. Write registry.json with version 1, active_workspace, and an unrelated sentinel object but no deeper_coverage_nudges marker. Invoke job-search (first_run:false), inspect the home and registry before answering the nudge, then answer 'maybe later' and inspect registry.json again.
expectations: During Gather reads the newest run's eligibility flag, zero-unseen evidence, eligible stream list, and the registry marker for the absolute workspace before deciding whether to render the nudge

Renders one concise nudge explaining that this healthy first-page run found no unseen roles while deeper Ashby results were available; it offers deeper company-board coverage without exposing cursors or page-browser jargon
Before an enablement, explains the additional-call model (one per continuation board page and full-posting read) and does not write search.max_new_postings_per_run merely because the nudge displayed

Immediately after rendering and before awaiting an answer, atomically merges one marker at deeper_coverage_nudges[<absolute workspace>] with matching workspace, an ISO shown_at, and provisional outcome deferred

After the user leaves it for later, the same marker remains deferred; the registry stays valid JSON, preserves version 1, active_workspace, and the unrelated sentinel object, and contains no duplicate marker for the workspace

evals.scenario: ineligible deeper-coverage nudge — no display and no marker write
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED three independent returning-workspace arms. In sandbox A, the newest healthy first-page run has unique_unseen_roles_first_pages 2 and deeper_coverage_nudge_eligible false even though an Ashby stream has has_more_at_stop true. In sandbox B, it has zero unseen roles but every board stream has has_more_at_stop false and deeper_coverage_nudge_eligible false. For arm C, make two otherwise-identical sandbox copies whose newest run has the exact eligible evidence from case 11; set search.max_new_postings_per_run: 50 in one and exact all in the other. Every registry has version 1, active_workspace, an unrelated sentinel key, and no deeper_coverage_nudges entry. Invoke job-search home once in A, B, and both C variants and inspect every registry.
expectations: Renders no deeper-coverage nudge in sandbox A because the latest run found unseen first-page roles
Renders no deeper-coverage nudge in sandbox B because no healthy cursor-capable stream reported trustworthy has_more true
Renders no deeper-coverage nudge in either arm C variant because current config already saves finite or all review depth, even though the latest first-page evidence is otherwise eligible
Does not synthesize eligibility from old runs, a non-empty stream list, duplicates, or intuition; the latest run's explicit evidence controls
Writes no deeper_coverage_nudges marker in any registry and leaves every registry file byte-for-byte unchanged
Still renders the ordinary returning-user home in all sandboxes

evals.scenario: declined, deferred, and unanswered deeper-coverage nudges never repeat
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED three independent returning workspaces A, B, and C with the exact eligible latest-run evidence from case 11 and no marker. Invoke home in A, answer 'no thanks', inspect the marker, then invoke home again. Invoke home in B, answer 'maybe later', inspect the marker, then invoke home again. Invoke home in C, inspect the marker as soon as the nudge renders, end the interaction without answering, then invoke home again. Keep a sentinel key in each registry to detect non-merge writes.
expectations: The first home in each workspace renders the eligible nudge exactly once and immediately atomically creates one provisional deferred marker before awaiting a reply
Workspace A updates its existing marker to declined; workspace B leaves its existing marker deferred; unanswered workspace C leaves its provisional marker deferred without a duplicate
Each marker has workspace equal to its map key and an ISO shown_at, while version 1, active_workspace, and the sentinel key are preserved
The second home in all three workspaces reads the present marker and stays quiet even though the latest run remains eligible, including after C's first interaction ended unanswered
No declined, deferred, or unanswered path writes search.max_new_postings_per_run or invokes job-search-run; the user can still request depth later

evals.scenario: enabled deeper-coverage nudge — exact saved scope confirms before config and outcome marker
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a returning workspace with the exact eligible latest-run evidence from case 11. Config has two enabled queries, search.sources [linkedin, ashby], schedule.frequency daily, version 1, and no max_new_postings_per_run. Registry has active_workspace, scheduling {installed:true, mechanism:'loop', set_at:'<ISO>'}, and a sentinel key but no nudge marker. Invoke home and inspect the registry as soon as the nudge appears. Then say 'review up to 50 every run.' Withhold yes until the preview finishes and inspect config/registry; then answer yes and inspect both files. Invoke home a second time.
expectations: Before yes, identifies the requested scope as saved finite depth, previews exactly 4 first-page metered search calls per run (2 enabled queries x 2 enabled sources) and about 120 first-page calls per 30-day month at the daily cadence (4 x 30), and states unknown continuation/detail additions
Before yes, explains that 50 bounds unique roles reviewed rather than page calls and asks for confirmation of the exact recurring target

As soon as the nudge renders, atomically creates one provisional deferred marker; before yes, config.yaml remains unchanged and that same marker is not enabled; only after yes is search.max_new_postings_per_run: 50 written atomically

After yes, atomically updates the same deeper_coverage_nudges[<absolute workspace>] marker to outcome enabled without duplicating it, retaining matching workspace and ISO shown_at while preserving registry version, active_workspace, installed scheduling marker, and sentinel key
The second home reads the marker and does not repeat the nudge; later scheduled runs use the saved scope without another confirmation
No budget, credits, or cost config field is added and no account balance or actual charge is inferred

evals.scenario: passive version-1 home read preserves bytes and creates no binding
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a returning workspace with a valid version-1 config containing a balanced legacy selector, a sentinel comment, and an unrelated sentinel field. Seed preferences/jobs/digest plus registry active_workspace, a sentinel key, and a fresh update_check cache with checked_at equal to the current UTC ISO and status failed; leave runs/detail-model-binding.json absent. Snapshot exact config bytes, the complete runs directory, and registry bytes. Symlink <repo>/tests/fake-host-capabilities into <tmp>/_bin, prepend it to PATH, and invoke job-search only to render home; do not request a v2-required action.
expectations: The passive home read renders the ordinary returning-user home without resolving a host model, migrating config, or prompting about migration

The fresh update_check cache suppresses an update fetch or cache write; config.yaml, registry, and every pre-existing artifact remain byte-for-byte unchanged, and the sentinel comment, config field, and registry key remain present
runs/detail-model-binding.json remains absent and no runs/config-backups directory is created
No fake-host-capabilities model, preflight, canary, or job operation occurs; passive compatibility is proved by the empty host log and artifact snapshots

evals.scenario: version-1 schedule request migrates transactionally in its single schedule confirmation
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a returning version-1 workspace as in case 15, with daily cadence, two enabled queries, two enabled sources, exact original-byte snapshot, no binding sidecar, and a registry sentinel. Symlink fake-host-capabilities into <tmp>/_bin, prepend it to PATH, set JOBSEARCH_TEST_HOST_SCENARIO=happy plus isolated host state/log, and ask to create the daily schedule. Use fake-host-capabilities utc-now; pre-create runs/config-backups/2026-07-16T12-00-00Z-config-v1.yaml with COLLISION-SENTINEL so exclusive-create collision handling is observable. Withhold yes until the complete single schedule confirmation, then approve. After yes drive the transaction snapshot through begin-migration, candidate validation through validate-candidate, and pair activation through activate-pair. All host model, free-preflight, disabled-job, canary, rollback, and cutoff effects must use the fixture; canonical run lifecycle evidence uses the bundled lifecycle mechanics, all job data calls use fake-agent-data, and no real scheduler or network is touched.
expectations: Before yes, one single schedule confirmation names cadence and machine change, removal path, exact primary model and exact detail model returned by fake-host-capabilities, the version-1 to version-2 migration, backup, calls-first recurring/canary context, one canary, and rollback; there is no separate migration prompt or model-choice ceremony
Before yes config, binding, registry, scheduler fixture state, call log, and collision backup are unchanged
After yes the complete candidate preserves the sentinel comment and unrelated field, replaces only version and required model material, and validate-candidate passes before activation
The original bytes are exclusively saved under runs/config-backups with a fresh UTC-safe timestamp; the pre-existing COLLISION-SENTINEL backup is never overwritten, truncated, or reused

begin-migration snapshots exact prior config/sidecar state; activate-pair makes config and a fresh matching runs/detail-model-binding.json one validated transaction, and free-preflight reports zero metered calls before register-disabled and canary

Only after fake-host-capabilities qualify-cutoff <workspace> <run_id> locates and folds the canonical ledger, exact run record/digest, active config, and canonical sidecar does the matching binding id/model establish and persist the first successful version-2 run cutoff; the fixture job then becomes eligible for the later scheduling flow, while this T3.2 fixture claims no general scheduler fidelity
No raw migration class or invented E-* migration code appears in normal chat, home, or digest

evals.scenario: version-1 migration candidate validation failure leaves the baseline untouched
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED the same version-1 schedule-request workspace and snapshots as case 16, with no sidecar. Set JOBSEARCH_TEST_HOST_SCENARIO=candidate-validation-failure and approve the complete single schedule confirmation. Drive validation through fake-host-capabilities validate-candidate. Inspect exact bytes, sidecar, backups, host state/log, registry, and fake agent-data call log.
expectations: The deterministic validation failure occurs before backup, activation, free preflight, canary, registry schedule write, or any metered call
Original config bytes remain exact, runs/detail-model-binding.json remains absent, no new config-backups file is created, and no test job exists
Normal user output states the observed validation cause, that the version-1 workspace was preserved, the next step, and exact conversational fix without a raw internal class or E-* migration code

evals.scenario: version-1 migration canary failure rolls back the pair and removes the new test job
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED and approve as in case 16, but set JOBSEARCH_TEST_HOST_SCENARIO=canary-failure. Let begin-migration, candidate validation, non-clobbering backup, activate-pair, free-preflight, and register-disabled succeed; then drive the canary failure and exact restoration/job cleanup through fake-host-capabilities rollback-migration <workspace> <job_id>. Inspect exact original-byte snapshot, active config/sidecar, backup, host state/log, registry, and call log.
expectations: The canary failure is a blocked run and does not qualify as the first successful version-2 run cutoff

The migration transaction atomically restores exact original version-1 config bytes and prior sidecar absence, so the newly created runs/detail-model-binding.json is removed; the recovery backup remains and is byte-identical to v1

The host log proves rollback-migration restored the pair and removed the newly registered disabled test job (or left it disabled only when removal is unavailable); registry never claims an installed or verified schedule
No later operation guesses success from registration, process status, or a started/blocked run; user output states cause, restored work, next step, and fix without a raw migration code

evals.scenario: first successful version-2 run fixes the rollback cutoff against later failure
evals.prompt: Complete the happy migration from case 16 and retain its exact v1 backup, migration snapshot, active v2 config, matching binding id/model, complete nonblocked run record, canonical lifecycle ledger, exact final digest, host log, and registry. First show that a started-only or blocked ledger does not qualify. Show that the old caller-supplied run/digest/binding/true qualify-cutoff shape is rejected, then call fake-host-capabilities qualify-cutoff <workspace> <run_id>; it must locate and fold canonical evidence itself. Exercise an unrelated version-2 run without the matching migration snapshot plus missing/mismatched ledger, run_id, run-record model/binding, digest, and active config arms before the complete matching arm. After the cutoff is persisted, prove that the persisted cutoff marker is an index by re-folding canonical evidence during rollback: a foreign forged marker cannot block legitimate pre-cutoff rollback, while a matching marker with unavailable canonical evidence must fail closed without restoring v1. Then invoke rollback-migration with intact cutoff evidence and inject a later preflight or canary failure; inspect every file.
expectations: Caller-supplied completion, a merely started or blocked ledger, missing/mismatched ledger or run_id, missing completed_at, mismatched config/sidecar/run binding or model, or missing exact final artifact makes qualify-cutoff fail and never establishes the rollback cutoff

The first successful version-2 run qualifies only when qualify-cutoff derives lifecycle can_complete=true from the canonical workspace ledger, observes the exact canonical record/digest plus active config/sidecar equality, and binds them to the exact fresh pair stored by this migration snapshot; it persists that binding/model/run cutoff

rollback-migration revalidates a matching marker against the canonical lifecycle/config/binding artifacts; it ignores a foreign marker for another migration, and a matching but unverifiable marker must fail closed without restoring version 1

After that cutoff, rollback-migration refuses restoration: later failures preserve the active version-2 config and matching binding evidence and never restore the stale version-1 backup; repair remains a version-2 action
The backup stays immutable recovery history, the failed new test job is removed or disabled, and no normal user surface leaks a raw migration class or invented E-* code

evals.scenario: rollback restores an exact prior sidecar and removes the registered job
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a version-1 workspace with exact original config bytes and an unexpected prior sidecar containing a prior sidecar byte sentinel. Set an isolated happy fixture state/log. Drive begin-migration, validate-candidate, activate-pair, and register-disabled; then set JOBSEARCH_TEST_HOST_SCENARIO=canary-failure, invoke canary, and call rollback-migration <workspace> <job_id>. Compare exact bytes and host state/log.
expectations: begin-migration records prior sidecar present and retains its exact bytes as rollback state
rollback-migration restores the exact original version-1 config and exact prior sidecar bytes rather than deleting, validating, or rewriting that prior file
The registered disabled fixture job is removed, with job_state absent in the rollback result and cleanup evidence in the host log
No registry state claims a verified schedule and the non-clobbering config backup remains recovery evidence

evals.scenario: register failure rolls back the activated pair before canary
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a version-1 workspace with no prior sidecar and exact byte snapshots. Drive begin-migration, validate-candidate, activate-pair, and free-preflight successfully, then set JOBSEARCH_TEST_HOST_SCENARIO=register-failure and call register-disabled followed by rollback-migration <workspace> <job_id>. Inspect files, state, and ordered host log.
expectations: The register failure occurs after the free preflight and before any canary, verified-schedule registry write, or metered call
rollback-migration restores exact version-1 config bytes and removes the newly activated binding because the prior sidecar was absent
Cleanup reports job_state not_created and fixture state contains no scheduler job; no remove success is invented for a job that was never created
User rendering reports the setup cause, restored work, next step, and fix without a raw migration code

evals.scenario: partial activation failure restores the exact pre-migration pair
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a version-1 workspace and exact snapshots, drive begin-migration plus validate-candidate, then set JOBSEARCH_TEST_HOST_SCENARIO=partial-activation-failure and invoke activate-pair followed by rollback-migration <workspace> <job_id>. Inspect the intermediate and restored files plus state/log.
expectations: activate-pair deterministically fails after replacing config only and reports active_effect config_only, so partial activation is executable rather than narrative
Before rollback the v2 candidate config is active while the new binding is absent; this partial pair is never treated as runnable or successful
rollback-migration restores exact version-1 config bytes and prior sidecar state, with job_state not_created because registration never ran
No free preflight, registration, canary, registry schedule write, or metered call occurs after partial activation failure

evals.scenario: rollback leaves the disabled job when removal is unavailable
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED and activate as in case 18, register the fixture job disabled, then set JOBSEARCH_TEST_HOST_SCENARIO=remove-unavailable and invoke rollback-migration <workspace> <job_id>. Inspect exact pair bytes, fixture state, host log, and registry.
expectations: rollback-migration restores the exact pre-migration config/sidecar state before reporting cleanup
Removal unavailable is explicit fixture evidence: rollback reports job_state disabled and the fixture state retains the same job disabled
The registry never claims an installed or verified schedule, and normal output states that the disabled job remains plus the exact removal follow-up
No generic scheduler fidelity is claimed from this narrow removal unavailable control

evals.scenario: version-2 candidate validation honors defaults and rejects malformed present values
evals.prompt: Build a minimal version-2 migration candidate with version, one query containing only id and keywords, search.detail_model, and its fresh exact matching binding. Deliberately leave omitted optional workspace, schedule, notify, search.sources, query location, query limit, and query enabled fields omitted. Validate it through fake-host-capabilities validate-candidate and prove the candidate bytes remain unchanged. In independent invalid arms, set search.max_new_postings_per_run to null, zero, negative, a float, a numeric string, and true. Also run valid arms with a positive integer or exact `all`. Inspect fixture output and candidate bytes; perform no activation, scheduler, network, or metered call.
expectations: The minimal candidate with omitted optional fields validates without materializing defaults or rewriting its bytes
Every malformed max_new_postings_per_run arm is rejected before activation, while a positive integer or exact `all` validates
The fresh binding must still have the complete canonical shape and exact id/model equality with the candidate
No backup, active config change, scheduler operation, network operation, or metered call occurs

evals.scenario: primary-only exact-model repair preserves the detail pair
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a version-2 workspace, canonical detail sidecar, unverified registry, and disabled fake scheduler job using the T3.3 fixture state. Set scenario expired-primary. Ask interactively to repair the scheduled search, accept the displayed defaults once, and drive repair-candidate, begin-repair, authorize-repair, activate-repair, and one repair-canary through fake-host-capabilities. Compare exact config/sidecar bytes and host state before and after.
expectations: The proposal preserves the available detail model exactly and changes only the primary to the repair session's observed exact model with origin repair_session
One confirmation covers both exact before/after slots, scheduler effects, removal/rollback, and one calls-first canary
Config and binding sidecar remain byte-identical; the job and registry stay disabled/unverified until the green fake real-path canary
No live scheduler, network, model roster, or agent-data account is used

evals.scenario: detail-only exact-model repair writes a fresh canonical binding
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED the same safe disabled/unverified version-2 state, set scenario expired-detail, and ask interactively to repair it. Accept the displayed default once and drive the full fake repair transaction and canary. Inspect exact primary metadata, config, new sidecar, and job state.
expectations: The valid primary and its origin are preserved while the unavailable detail defaults to the least-powerful available adequate judgment model
The detail write creates a fresh complete sidecar with a fresh binding id/timestamp and origin repair
No enable/verified claim occurs before one green real-path canary; that canary is the only commit point
Normal output gives the observed cause and conversational repair without a raw internal class

evals.scenario: both expired exact-model defaults use their distinct origins
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED the repair state, set scenario both-expired, and ask to repair it. Capture the proposal before answering, then approve once and drive the fixture through a green canary.
expectations: Both exact before/after slots are shown; primary defaults to the exact repair-session model with repair_session origin and detail defaults independently with repair origin
The complete machine/config/binding/rollback effects and canonical calls-first context precede exactly one confirmation
The job stays disabled and registry unverified during staging and activates only after the green canary
No prefix, tier label, guessed identifier, or host auto-substitution is accepted

evals.scenario: exact override wins while unknown and ambiguous overrides are rejected
evals.prompt: Using isolated repair-candidate fixture state with alternate prior origins (repair_session/configured_user and user_override/repair), first resolve without overrides and prove unchanged available slots preserve those origins. Then request one different exact available primary override and detail override, request the same exact primary identifier as an explicit override, and independently try an unknown identifier and an ambiguous prefix. Do not authorize a transaction for rejected candidates.
expectations: Each exact available user identifier wins over the automatic default
Without an override, alternate prior origins are preserved; selecting the same exact primary is accepted and explicitly rebinds its origin to user_override
Unknown, unavailable, and ambiguous-prefix selections are rejected before confirmation or mutation
A primary override records user_override; any approved detail binding write records repair in its fresh sidecar
No rejected arm changes config, sidecar, registry, scheduler state, or call log

evals.scenario: repair rollback restores every surface and a retry needs fresh consent
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED exact byte snapshots, approve a both-slot fake repair once, then run two isolated failure arms: repair-partial-activation-failure during activation and repair-canary-failure after staging. Compare config, sidecar, registry, and fake job state. Attempt another canary without beginning and authorizing a fresh transaction.
expectations: Both failure arms automatically restore exact prior config, sidecar, registry, scheduler metadata, and verification state with no proposed model active
The repaired job remains disabled and unverified after rollback
A retry without fresh calls-first context and one fresh scoped confirmation is rejected
User rendering states observed cause, restored state, next step, and exact conversational fix without a raw internal class
